summary: A 2021 musical with music by Jeanine Tesori and book and lyrics by David Lindsay-Abaire, based on Lindsay-Abaire's 2000 play. Won the 2023 Tony Award for Best Musical. The story follows Kimberly Levaco, a teenager in New Jersey who has a condition that causes her to age rapidly. Victoria Clark won a Tony for originating the role of Kimberly. The musical features her con-artist aunt Debra, a love interest who brainstorms anagrams of her name, and a number framed as a letter to the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

works.name: Plot and Characters
works.description: Kimberly Levaco is a teenager who ages rapidly due to a medical condition. She is repeatedly perplexed by references to 'what happened in Secaucus.' Her aunt Debra is a con artist who once lived in a library after a coyote set fire to her tent; Kimberly listens to Debra dragging a USPS collection box down the stairs. Kimberly has a birthday party at a place called Skater Planet in the number 'Better.' Her love interest brainstorms anagrams of her name, eventually landing on one that uses the word 'Akimbo.' She is told 'you gotta hustle hard, you gotta muscle through' by a character who announces a show choir's sexual orientations. Wikipedia

works.name: General / Identification
works.description: The musical is composed by Jeanine Tesori (who also composed Fun Home and Shrek the Musical) with book and lyrics by David Lindsay-Abaire. Won the 2023 Tony Award for Best Musical. Tesori and Lindsay-Abaire shared the 2023 Tony for Best Original Score. Victoria Clark was nominated for (and won) a Tony for playing the title role of a rapidly-aging teenager. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Won the 2023 Tony Award for Best Musical; Tesori and Lindsay-Abaire won for Best Original Score

comprehensive_summary:
Kimberly Akimbo is a very recent addition to the quizbowl canon, having won the 2023 Tony Award for Best Musical. It has appeared in only a handful of questions so far, primarily at higher difficulties. The musical was composed by Jeanine Tesori (known for Fun Home, Shrek, and Thoroughly Modern Millie) with book and lyrics by David Lindsay-Abaire.

The single existing tossup is extremely detailed, targeting specific plot points: Kimberly's confusion about 'what happened in Secaucus,' her aunt Debra's antics (dragging a USPS box, the coyote-and-tent backstory), the Make-A-Wish letter requesting a monkey and treehouse, the anagram of her name that yields 'Akimbo,' and her birthday at Skater Planet in the number 'Better.' Victoria Clark's Tony-winning performance is also clued.

In bonus form, it appears as a Jeanine Tesori identifier alongside Fun Home and Shrek. Given its recency and critical acclaim, this topic is likely to grow in frequency.

Fun Home — another Tesori musical, based on Alison Bechdel's graphic novel
David Lindsay-Abaire — book and lyrics for Kimberly Akimbo
